title: IOSH Managing Safely
headline: The benchmark safety qualification for line managers and supervisors.
learningOutcomes: Identify workplace hazards and assess risk using a structured methodology
Apply control-of-work principles to reduce incidents and downtime
Communicate safety responsibilities confidently across a team
Investigate incidents and close out corrective actions effectively

moduleOutline.title: Introducing Managing Safely
moduleOutline.description: The manager's legal and moral responsibilities, and the business case for safety.

moduleOutline.title: Assessing risks
moduleOutline.description: Structured risk assessment, ALARP principle and risk registers.

moduleOutline.title: Controlling risks
moduleOutline.description: Hierarchy of control, safe systems of work and permit-to-work fundamentals.

moduleOutline.title: Understanding responsibilities
moduleOutline.description: UK, GCC and international HSE duties for managers.

moduleOutline.title: Understanding hazards
moduleOutline.description: Chemical, physical, biological, ergonomic and psychosocial hazards.

moduleOutline.title: Investigating incidents
moduleOutline.description: Root-cause analysis, learning reviews and corrective action tracking.

moduleOutline.title: Measuring performance
moduleOutline.description: Leading and lagging indicators, audits and continuous improvement.

assessment: 40-question written assessment plus a workplace-based risk assessment project.
certification: IOSH Managing Safely certificate, recognised globally and valid for life.

title: IOSH Working Safely
headline: Core safety awareness for every worker — in any industry, anywhere in the world.
learningOutcomes: Understand why safety is everyone's responsibility
Recognise common workplace hazards across sectors
Use safe behaviours to protect yourself and colleagues
Report concerns through the right channels

moduleOutline.title: Introducing Working Safely
moduleOutline.description: Why safety matters and the cost of getting it wrong.

moduleOutline.title: Defining hazard and risk
moduleOutline.description: How to spot hazards in your own role.

moduleOutline.title: Identifying common hazards
moduleOutline.description: Slips, trips, manual handling, chemical, noise, stress.

moduleOutline.title: Improving safety performance
moduleOutline.description: Personal contribution to a safer workplace.

moduleOutline.title: Protecting the environment
moduleOutline.description: Everyday actions to reduce environmental impact.

assessment: Multiple-choice assessment plus a hazard-spotting exercise.
certification: IOSH Working Safely certificate.

title: NEBOSH International General Certificate
headline: The world's most respected health and safety qualification for practitioners.
learningOutcomes: Apply risk management and control strategies in any sector
Interpret international standards including ILO OSH-2001 and ISO 45001
Investigate incidents and design robust safe systems of work
Prepare for the NEBOSH IG1 and IG2 assessments
whoShouldAttend: Safety officers seeking a recognised qualification

moduleOutline.title: IG1 — Management of international health and safety
moduleOutline.description: 11 elements covering policy, culture, risk and performance.

moduleOutline.title: IG2 — Risk assessment
moduleOutline.description: Practical workplace-based risk assessment project.

assessment: IG1 open-book written assessment and IG2 practical risk assessment.
certification: NEBOSH International General Certificate.

title: OTHM Level 6 Diploma in Occupational Health and Safety
headline: A regulated Level 6 diploma with direct progression to a UK master's degree.
learningOutcomes: Lead enterprise-wide safety strategy and culture programmes
Design management systems aligned to ISO 45001
Influence board-level risk appetite and assurance
Progress to MSc Occupational Health and Safety with UK partners

moduleOutline.title: Health and safety management principles and policy
moduleOutline.description: Enterprise HSE policy architecture.

moduleOutline.title: Risk and incident management
moduleOutline.description: Advanced risk frameworks, HAZOP, bow-tie analysis.

moduleOutline.title: Promoting a positive health and safety culture
moduleOutline.description: Behavioural safety and leadership.

moduleOutline.title: Maintaining a safe working environment
moduleOutline.description: Technical control strategies across hazards.

moduleOutline.title: Workplace law
moduleOutline.description: UK and GCC comparative health and safety law.

moduleOutline.title: Research project
moduleOutline.description: Workplace-based independent research dissertation.

assessment: Six written assignments plus an applied research project.
certification: OTHM Level 6 Diploma, regulated by Ofqual.

title: HABC Level 2 Award in Food Safety in Catering
headline: An Ofqual-regulated food safety qualification for catering teams.
learningOutcomes: Understand food safety hazards and how to control them
Apply HACCP principles in day-to-day kitchen operations
Maintain personal, kitchen and equipment hygiene
Comply with food safety law in your region

moduleOutline.title: Food safety legislation
moduleOutline.description: Regional food safety duties and enforcement.

moduleOutline.title: Microbiological hazards
moduleOutline.description: Bacteria, viruses, parasites and toxin control.

moduleOutline.title: HACCP fundamentals
moduleOutline.description: The seven principles applied to catering operations.

certification: HABC Level 2 Award, Ofqual regulated.

title: Fire Marshal & Warden
headline: Practical fire prevention, evacuation and emergency command for every workplace.
learningOutcomes: Identify and control fire hazards
Lead evacuations with confidence
Use portable fire-fighting equipment correctly
Coordinate with emergency services during an incident

moduleOutline.title: Fire science and behaviour
moduleOutline.description: Fire tetrahedron, spread and smoke control.

moduleOutline.title: Fire prevention
moduleOutline.description: Housekeeping, hot work permits and ignition control.

moduleOutline.title: Emergency response
moduleOutline.description: Evacuation routes, refuge points and assembly.

moduleOutline.title: Practical drill
moduleOutline.description: Hands-on use of extinguishers and fire blankets.

assessment: Written assessment plus supervised practical drill.
certification: OSHAwards Fire Marshal certificate.

title: IEMA Foundation Certificate in Environmental Management
headline: Build the environmental and sustainability fluency that every business now requires.
learningOutcomes: Understand the principles of environmental management
Interpret ISO 14001 and plan for implementation
Communicate environmental performance to stakeholders
Support a net-zero and ESG reporting strategy

moduleOutline.title: Principles of environmental management
moduleOutline.description: Policy, legislation and stakeholders.

moduleOutline.title: Environmental management systems
moduleOutline.description: Plan-Do-Check-Act and ISO 14001.

moduleOutline.title: Sustainable operations
moduleOutline.description: Energy, waste, water and resource efficiency.

assessment: Portfolio of workplace tasks and a written assessment.
certification: IEMA Foundation Certificate.

title: First Aid at Work (Level 3)
headline: A regulated first-aid qualification for nominated responders in any workplace.
learningOutcomes: Assess an incident scene safely
Provide effective CPR and use an AED
Manage bleeding, shock, burns, fractures and medical emergencies
Complete a compliant incident record

moduleOutline.title: Primary and secondary survey
moduleOutline.description: Scene safety and patient assessment.

moduleOutline.title: CPR & AED
moduleOutline.description: Adult, child and infant protocols.

moduleOutline.title: Trauma management
moduleOutline.description: Bleeding, fractures, burns, head and spinal injury.

moduleOutline.title: Medical emergencies
moduleOutline.description: Cardiac, diabetic, seizure, stroke and anaphylaxis.

assessment: Practical skills assessment plus a written paper.
certification: HABC Level 3 Award in First Aid at Work.
